summary: No concrete P0/P1 acceptance blocker found in the assigned packet, referenced run artifacts, workflow definition, review-control docs, external-reviewer wrapper, review-set runner, or PR-readiness evaluator. The current run is correctly still in provider_mirrored_review, does not claim merge_ready, has no final readiness report, has no human decision artifact, and labels mock Claude smoke evidence as wrapper-only rather than live review evidence. The PR-readiness workflow/evaluator also contains explicit blockers for missing live Claude evidence, stale review evidence, unhandled P0/P1 source findings, missing collision-control evidence, sensitive raw-output mishandling, and missing human merge approval. I found no grounded path where the current artifacts could validly claim accepted_merge_ready before live review, finding validation, collision handling if needed, and human approval complete.
self_declared_limitations: Read-only review only; I did not run tests, execute scripts, modify files, create patches, or update evidence.
I did not inspect raw Claude provider output contents, because raw external output can be sensitive and the metadata was sufficient for this authority-boundary review.

Live Claude invocation/report artifacts appeared in the run directory after the assigned packet was prepared, but no fusion report, finding-validation report, human decision, or pr-merge-readiness report existed in the inspected run root; I treated the run as still in provider_mirrored_review rather than accepted evidence.
I did not rely on other reviewers' substantive findings for this report; conclusions above are grounded in the assigned packet, repository files, and referenced metadata inspected directly.
